title: Visa backs Open USD with new stablecoin platform as Circle faces fresh competition
content: Visa launched a stablecoin platform that lets banks and fintechs issue, manage and settle digital dollars through its payments network.
summary: Visa has launched a new stablecoin platform that enables banks and fintechs to issue, manage, and settle digital dollars using its existing payments network. This move introduces significant competition for established stablecoin providers like Circle.

title: Crypto for Advisors: Strengthening defenses against AI fraud
content: Strengthening defenses against AI fraud
summary: This article discusses the critical need for strengthening defenses against AI-powered fraud, specifically tailored for crypto advisors. It aims to provide strategies and insights for the crypto community to protect against sophisticated AI-driven scams and threats in the evolving digital landscape.

title: U.S. adds four Iran central bank crypto wallets to sanctions, Tether freezes $131 million of contents
content: The freeze targets TRON-based addresses that held over $165 million, preventing those specific funds from being transferred or redeemed.
summary: The U.S. has sanctioned four crypto wallets linked to Iran's central bank, prompting Tether to freeze $131 million of their contents. These TRON-based addresses, which collectively held over $165 million, are now blocked from transferring or redeeming the funds.

title: Cantor and Securitize collaborate on blockchain-based IPOs
content: The move creates a pathway for public companies to raise capital onchain and issue tokenized securities.
summary: Cantor Fitzgerald and Securitize have partnered to introduce blockchain-based Initial Public Offerings (IPOs). This collaboration will enable public companies to raise capital onchain and issue tokenized securities, integrating blockchain technology into traditional capital markets.

title: $1.9 trillion asset manager T. Rowe Price bets on active management with first multi-token crypto ETF
content: T. Rowe Price launched what it says is the industry's first actively managed multi-token spot crypto ETF, offering diversified exposure to digital assets.
summary: T. Rowe Price, a major $1.9 trillion asset manager, has launched what it describes as the industry's first actively managed multi-token spot crypto ETF. This new exchange-traded fund is designed to offer diversified exposure to various digital assets.

title: Citadel Securities invests $400 million in Crypto.com, valuing exchange at $20 billion
content: The exchange's first institutional funding round values it at $20 billion and will fund expansion into tokenized securities and derivatives.
summary: Citadel Securities has invested $400 million in Crypto.com, marking the exchange's first institutional funding round and valuing it at $20 billion. This significant investment will fund Crypto.com's expansion into tokenized securities and derivatives.

title: Stripe’s $53 billion PayPal bid is a high-stakes play to own the future of digital payments
content: If Stripe acquires PayPal, the real prize could be consumer wallets, stablecoin issuance or the infrastructure powering the next generation of digital payments.
summary: Stripe's potential $53 billion bid for PayPal is a strategic move to secure its dominance in the future of digital payments. The acquisition's main value could stem from owning consumer wallets, engaging in stablecoin issuance, or providing the foundational infrastructure for next-generation digital transactions.

title: Crypto brokerage firm Alpaca raises $135 million for tokenized stock infrastructure
content: The company has previously cleared or held in custody roughly 94% of tokenized U.S. equities and now holds over $1.5 billion in underlying stocks.
summary: Crypto brokerage firm Alpaca has secured $135 million in funding to advance its tokenized stock infrastructure. The company has previously been responsible for clearing or holding in custody approximately 94% of tokenized U.S. equities and currently manages over $1.5 billion in underlying stocks.

title: Ledger wants AI agents to manage crypto without holding your keys
content: AI agents can read wallet balances and analyze portfolios but requires every sensitive action to be approved on a Ledger hardware device before it can be executed.
summary: Ledger is developing a system that allows AI agents to manage cryptocurrency portfolios, enabling them to read balances and analyze holdings. However, this system ensures that all sensitive actions require explicit approval on a Ledger hardware device, preventing AI from ever holding private keys.

title: Galaxy targets institutional stablecoin yield with new DeFi vaults
content: The crypto financial services firm launched Galaxy Curator, a Morpho-based platform that gives Fireblocks' 2,400 institutional clients access to onchain yield strategies.
summary: Galaxy, a crypto financial services firm, has launched Galaxy Curator, a Morpho-based platform. This new offering provides Fireblocks' 2,400 institutional clients with access to onchain yield strategies, specifically targeting institutional stablecoin yield.

title: The most popular bitcoin call option has slipped by $10,000
content: Your day-ahead look for July 18, 2026
summary: The most popular bitcoin call option has slipped by $10,000, indicating a shift in market sentiment. This change may impact investor expectations and bitcoin's price. The development will be closely watched by the crypto community.

title: Inside the high-stakes race to build an open financial system specifically for AI bots
content: The newly-convened x402 Foundation is a neutral space where competitors and payment methods can work together to create an open standard for AI agentic commerce.
summary: The x402 Foundation aims to create an open standard for AI agentic commerce, bringing together competitors and payment methods to work towards this goal. This endeavor seeks to build an open financial system specifically designed for AI bots. The foundation provides a neutral space for collaboration and innovation.

title: U.S. Senate unanimously opposes clemency for FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried
content: The nonbinding resolution passed without objection after Bankman-Fried asked for clemency, months after Trump pardoned other major crypto figures, including Changpeng Zhao and Ross Ulbricht.
summary: The U.S. Senate unanimously passed a nonbinding resolution opposing clemency for FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried, following his request. This decision comes months after former President Trump pardoned other prominent crypto figures, including Changpeng Zhao and Ross Ulbricht.

title: A bitcoin wallet dormant since the 2017 peak just moved $383 million
content: The coins went to a fresh address rather than an exchange, so nothing has been sold yet.
summary: A bitcoin wallet that had been dormant since the 2017 market peak recently moved $383 million in coins. The funds were transferred to a new address, indicating they have not yet been sold on an exchange.

title: Two groups of crypto investors are selling bitcoin to cap its latest price recovery
content: Bitcoin surges toward $65,000 on softer-than-expected inflation data, but on-chain signals show two key investor groups selling into the bounce.
summary: Bitcoin prices have surged to nearly $65,000 due to softer-than-expected US inflation data, but two key investor groups are selling into the bounce, as indicated by on-chain signals. This selling activity suggests that some investors are taking profits despite the price increase. The surge in price is a result of the inflation data release.

title: DTCC moves tokenized securities into live trading, marking a milestone for Wall Street's blockchain push
content: DTCC's first live production trades using tokenized securities show how blockchain could reshape the infrastructure behind Wall Street.
summary: DTCC has successfully conducted its first live production trades using tokenized securities, marking a significant milestone for Wall Street's adoption of blockchain technology. This development demonstrates the potential for blockchain to reshape the underlying infrastructure of financial markets.

title: Ostium suffers $18 million exploit as oracle attack wave continues to hit DeFi
content: A hacker used Ostium's own price-reporting infrastructure against the protocol, submitting falsified future-dated oracle data to manufacture fake trading profits and trigger an $18 million payout.
summary: Ostium suffered an $18 million exploit due to an oracle attack, where a hacker manipulated the protocol's own price-reporting infrastructure with falsified future-dated data. This allowed the attacker to generate fake trading profits and trigger a large payout.

title: Bitcoin outlook improves amid 6% weekly gain: Can BTC bulls push higher?
content: Bitcoin buyers have returned to spot, futures and ETF markets but geopolitical headwinds could quickly unravel the progress of the past two weeks.
summary: Bitcoin has seen a 6% gain over the past week, but geopolitical tensions could impact its progress, despite buyers returning to spot, futures, and ETF markets. The outlook for Bitcoin has improved, but it remains to be seen if bulls can push the price higher. The recent progress is fragile and can be quickly undone by external factors.

title: Injective files for SEC transfer agent registration to bring securities ownership records onchain
content: The company said the filing would create a regulated pathway for maintaining ownership records for tokenized securities onchain.
summary: Injective has filed for SEC transfer agent registration, aiming to create a regulated pathway for onchain maintenance of tokenized securities ownership records. This move could bridge traditional securities and blockchain technology. The filing may facilitate more efficient and secure record-keeping for securities.

title: BitPay secures Dutch licensing under MiCA, plans to expand stablecoin payments
content: The Dutch Authority for the Financial Markets approved BitPay’s application as a crypto-asset service provider under the Markets in Crypto-Assets requirements.
summary: BitPay has received Dutch licensing under MiCA, allowing it to operate as a crypto-asset service provider. This approval enables the company to expand its stablecoin payment services. The move is expected to increase BitPay's presence in the European market.

title: Ethics in crypto market structure ‘really not our concern,’ says Blockchain Association CEO
content: A former CFTC commissioner urged lawmakers not to “kill all the hard work that we put in the rest of the bill” by making ethics a make-or-break issue on crypto market structure.
summary: The Blockchain Association CEO stated that ethics in crypto market structure is not their concern, while a former CFTC commissioner urged lawmakers not to prioritize ethics over other aspects of the bill. This discussion is part of a broader debate on crypto regulation. The CEO's comments may have implications for the future of crypto market structure.

title: Morgan Stanley’s E*TRADE launches spot crypto trading through Zero Hash
content: Eligible E*TRADE retail clients can now buy, sell and hold Bitcoin, Ether and Solana through a partnership with crypto infrastructure provider Zero Hash.
summary: Morgan Stanley's E*TRADE has launched spot crypto trading for eligible retail clients, allowing them to buy, sell, and hold Bitcoin, Ether, and Solana through a partnership with Zero Hash. This move expands E*TRADE's services into the cryptocurrency market. The partnership enables E*TRADE clients to access crypto trading capabilities.

title: Tradable’s $1B Stellar deal adds to institutional tokenization boom
content: Tradable will bring up to $1 billion in private credit assets to Stellar, extending the blockchain’s push into institutional finance and tokenized real-world assets.
summary: Tradable is partnering with Stellar to bring up to $1 billion in private credit assets to the blockchain, advancing institutional finance and tokenized assets. This deal contributes to the growing trend of institutional tokenization. The partnership aims to extend Stellar's reach into institutional finance.

title: Alpaca raises $135M to fund tokenized agent-first infrastructure
content: The BNP-backed brokerage infrastructure provider is expanding into tokenized markets and AI-native financial services as both DeFi and TradFi companies pursue onchain business.
summary: Alpaca, a BNP-backed brokerage infrastructure provider, has raised $135M to expand into tokenized markets and AI-native financial services, catering to both DeFi and TradFi companies. This move aims to support onchain business pursuits. The funding will help bridge the gap between traditional and decentralized finance.

title: Prediction markets defy crypto downturn with record Q2 volume: CoinGecko
content: Prediction markets reached a record $113.8 billion in notional volume in Q2 as spot CEX trading, derivatives volume and stablecoin market cap declined.
summary: Prediction markets reached a record $113.8 billion in notional volume in Q2, defying the crypto downturn. This growth occurred despite declines in spot CEX trading, derivatives volume, and stablecoin market cap. The record volume suggests increased interest in prediction markets.

title: MoonPay buys Glide to bolster crypto deposits infrastructure
content: MoonPay acquired Glide, a crypto infrastructure startup founded by former Robinhood Wallet engineers, to expand cross-chain crypto deposit infrastructure.
summary: MoonPay has acquired Glide, a crypto infrastructure startup, to expand its cross-chain crypto deposit infrastructure. Glide was founded by former Robinhood Wallet engineers. The acquisition aims to bolster MoonPay's crypto deposits infrastructure.

title: Tokenized stocks market cap gains to record $2.3B
content: The market capitalization of tokenized stocks rose to a record $2.3 billion, as investors sought exposure to the growing list of tokenized equity products launched by cryptocurrency exchanges.
summary: The market capitalization of tokenized stocks has reached a record $2.3 billion, driven by investor interest in tokenized equity products offered by cryptocurrency exchanges. This growth indicates a significant increase in demand for tokenized stocks. The rise of tokenized stocks has opened up new opportunities for investors to access traditional assets in a digital format.

title: FATF urges faster crypto AML enforcement as stablecoin crime increases
content: The global task force said criminal networks are using stablecoins and developing proprietary tokens to evade asset freezes as countries struggle to enforce crypto AML rules.
summary: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is urging countries to speed up the enforcement of anti-money laundering (AML) rules for cryptocurrencies, as criminal networks increasingly use stablecoins to evade asset freezes. Stablecoins are being used to develop proprietary tokens, making it harder for authorities to track transactions. This increase in stablecoin-related crime is causing concern for global regulators.

title: ARK pushes back against a16z’s ‘TradFi wants blockchain, not DeFi’ claim
content: ARK Invest’s director of research disputed a16z crypto’s thesis that traditional finance will adopt permissioned blockchain infrastructure instead of decentralized finance, saying institutions will increasingly rely on DeFi rails.
summary: ARK Invest's director of research disagrees with a16z crypto's claim that traditional finance will adopt permissioned blockchain infrastructure over decentralized finance, stating that institutions will rely on DeFi rails. This dispute highlights the ongoing debate between traditional and decentralized finance. The director's statement suggests a shift towards DeFi adoption in the financial sector.

title: US Senate unanimously adopts resolution opposing clemency for SBF
content: The Senate approved a resolution opposing clemency for former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried as prediction markets put the odds of a Trump pardon by July 31 below 1%.
summary: The US Senate has unanimously adopted a resolution opposing clemency for former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried. This move occurs as prediction markets indicate less than a 1% chance of a presidential pardon for SBF by July 31.

title: Bitcoin $107K buyers providing ‘early signals’ of 2026 bear-market bottom: Glassnode
content: Bitcoin realized losses appeared to be copying a reversal structure that marked previous bear-market bottoms, with $69,000 a new BTC price battleground.
summary: According to Glassnode, Bitcoin's realized losses are showing signs of a reversal structure similar to previous bear-market bottoms, with a potential battleground at $69,000. This could be an early signal of a bear-market bottom in 2026. Bitcoin buyers at $107,000 may be contributing to this trend.

title: Stanford study says 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets enable settlement manipulation
content: Researchers found that Polymarket’s five-minute Bitcoin prediction markets create incentives to manipulate spot prices around contract settlement, proposing longer settlement windows as a potential fix.
summary: A Stanford study found that 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets can be manipulated, creating incentives to influence spot prices around contract settlement. The researchers suggested longer settlement windows as a potential solution. This could impact the crypto market's integrity.

title: Revolut receives in-principle approval from UAE authorities for crypto services
content: Dubai’s Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority approved the London-headquartered fintech company for broker-dealer, management and investment, and exchange services in the UAE.
summary: Revolut has received in-principle approval from the UAE's Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority to offer crypto services, including broker-dealer, management, and exchange services. This approval allows Revolut to expand its cryptocurrency operations in the UAE. The London-based fintech company can now proceed with launching its crypto services in the region.

title: US senator blasts AG pick for ‘dismantling’ crypto unit, Trump’s CZ pardon
content: President Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general faced criticism from members of the Senate Judiciary Committee on his approach to crypto enforcement and the pardon of former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao.
summary: President Donald Trump's nominee for US Attorney General is facing criticism from the Senate Judiciary Committee regarding his approach to cryptocurrency enforcement. Senators expressed concerns over the alleged 'dismantling' of a crypto unit and the potential pardon of former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao.

title: Ostium pauses trading as security firms report multimillion-dollar oracle exploit
content: Ostium halted trading and advised revoking contract approvals after blockchain security firms reported an apparent oracle-related exploit of its OLP liquidity vault, with estimated losses ranging from $18 million to $22 million.
summary: Ostium has paused trading due to a reported oracle exploit of its OLP liquidity vault, with estimated losses between $18 million and $22 million. The company has advised users to revoke contract approvals. The exploit was discovered by blockchain security firms.

title: Aave launches V4 on Avalanche, laying groundwork for tokenized credit markets
content: The deployment marks Aave V4’s first expansion beyond Ethereum and introduces lending infrastructure designed to support future markets for tokenized real-world assets.
summary: Aave has launched V4 on Avalanche, expanding beyond Ethereum and introducing lending infrastructure to support future markets for tokenized real-world assets. This deployment marks a significant step in the development of tokenized credit markets. The move is expected to facilitate the growth of decentralized lending and borrowing on the Avalanche network.

title: JPMorgan says bitcoin outlook sees ‘encouraging sign’ as Strategy boosts cash reserves
content: Strategy's larger cash reserves and improving institutional demand in bitcoin futures are "encouraging signs" for bitcoin, JPMorgan said.
summary: JPMorgan views increasing cash reserves and institutional demand for bitcoin futures as positive signs for the cryptocurrency. This outlook suggests a potential boost for bitcoin. The improvement in investor sentiment may lead to increased adoption and investment.

title: ‘We’re going to get it done’: Crypto bill faces what could be final test over Trump conflicts of interest
content: Optimism is brewing to get a crypto bill passed, with some variations on timing as onlookers wait to see how ethics will shake out.
summary: A crypto bill is approaching a potential final test, with optimism growing that it will be passed, despite concerns over ethics and timing. The bill's outcome is uncertain, but onlookers are watching closely. Ethics and conflicts of interest may impact the bill's passage.

title: Citadel Securities invests $400 million in Crypto.com at $20 billion valuation
content: Crypto.com plans to use proceeds from its “first institutional funding round" to expand its tokenization and derivatives operations.
summary: Crypto.com has secured a $400 million investment from Citadel Securities, valuing the company at $20 billion, and plans to use the funds to expand its tokenization and derivatives operations. This investment marks Crypto.com's first institutional funding round. The deal highlights growing interest in cryptocurrency from major financial institutions.

title: $1.9 trillion asset manager T. Rowe Price launches first actively managed multi-token crypto ETF
content: T. Rowe Price's TKNZ Active Crypto ETF began trading Thursday after the firm filed for the product in October.
summary: T. Rowe Price, a $1.9 trillion asset manager, has launched its first actively managed multi-token crypto ETF, called TKNZ Active Crypto ETF, which began trading on Thursday. The firm had filed for the product in October. This move marks a significant entry into the crypto market by a major asset manager.

title: Visa launching internal stablecoin platform for clients that provides access to OUSD: report
content: Open Standard's stablecoin is expected to become a major competitor to Circle's USDC, the dominant stablecoin in the U.S.
summary: Visa is launching an internal stablecoin platform for its clients, which will provide access to Open Standard's stablecoin OUSD, potentially competing with Circle's USDC. This move could impact the stablecoin market in the US. The platform's launch is expected to have significant implications for the crypto industry.

title: US jury indicts South Dakota crypto investor on 29 counts over alleged $20 million investor fraud
content: DOJ indicted Sioux Falls crypto investor Benjamin Paul Wiener on 29 counts over an alleged $20 million fraud, with a trial set for Sept. 15.
summary: A US jury has indicted Benjamin Paul Wiener, a crypto investor from South Dakota, on 29 counts of alleged $20 million investor fraud, with a trial scheduled for September 15. Wiener's indictment is a significant development in the case. The trial will determine the outcome of the allegations against him.

title: SBI extends onchain finance push with Ondo deal to tokenize Japanese stocks
content: Japan's SBI Holdings will use JPYSC for settlement and collateral while distributing Ondo products through its network.
summary: SBI Holdings is partnering with Ondo to tokenize Japanese stocks, using JPYSC for settlement and collateral, and will distribute Ondo products through its network. This deal is part of SBI's push into onchain finance. The partnership aims to increase accessibility to Japanese stocks through tokenization.

title: Ledger unveils hardware-backed Agent Stack to prevent rogue AI transactions
content: With Ledger Agent Stack, users can deploy bots to read balances, suggest transactions, prepare swaps and draft operations.
summary: Ledger has introduced the Agent Stack, a hardware-backed solution that enables users to deploy bots for various transactions while preventing rogue AI activities. This innovation aims to enhance security and usability in crypto transactions. The Agent Stack allows for tasks such as reading balances and suggesting transactions.

title: Dune research finds 85% of concentrated DeFi liquidity is underutilized, with $150M in annual fees foregone
content: Dune research commissioned by 1inch finds 85% of concentrated DeFi liquidity underutilized, with $150M in annual fees forgone.
summary: A recent study by Dune research found that 85% of concentrated DeFi liquidity is underutilized, resulting in $150M in annual fees being forgone. The study was commissioned by 1inch. This underutilization indicates a significant missed opportunity for revenue in the DeFi space.

title: Jeff Booth, Lyn Alden-backed ORANGE JUICE raises $40 million to launch permanent capital firm with bitcoin treasury strategy
content: ORANGE JUICE raised $40 million to acquire businesses, pursue a bitcoin treasury strategy, and implement a permanent capital ownership model.
summary: ORANGE JUICE has raised $40 million to launch a permanent capital firm that will acquire businesses and implement a bitcoin treasury strategy. The firm is backed by notable investors including Jeff Booth and Lyn Alden. This funding will support the company's unique approach to investing and treasury management.

title: Lummis, Gallego secure unanimous Senate approval opposing Sam Bankman-Fried clemency
content: The U.S. Senate unanimously approved a resolution opposing a presidential pardon or commutation for convicted FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried.
summary: The US Senate has unanimously approved a resolution opposing a presidential pardon for Sam Bankman-Fried, the convicted founder of FTX. This decision reflects the Senate's stance on holding individuals accountable for their actions in the crypto space. The resolution is a significant development in the ongoing saga surrounding FTX's collapse.

title: Visa says stablecoins will power micro-commerce in AI agentic economy
content: Visa said it expects agentic commerce to integrate a hybrid flow that combines both card and stablecoin rails at different stages of a task.
summary: Visa expects stablecoins to play a key role in micro-commerce within an AI-driven economy, integrating both card and stablecoin payment rails. This hybrid approach aims to enhance transaction efficiency. The development of agentic commerce may revolutionize the way transactions are conducted.

Bitcoin Magazine

content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Asset manager T. Rowe Price has debuted a crypto fund giving investors exposure to Bitcoin and other digital assets.

This fund is expected to give investors a new way to engage with the crypto market. The launch is a notable move by a major asset manager into the crypto sector.

title: Fed Chair Warsh: No Bailout for Crypto Industry in Crisis
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Fed Chair Warsh: No Bailout for Crypto Industry in Crisis
Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said the Fed will not bail out failing crypto firms, insisting the industry must bear its own risks while regulators finalize rules under the GENIUS Act.
This post Fed Chair Warsh: No Bailout for Crypto Industry in Crisis first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh stated that the Fed will not provide a bailout for the crypto industry in times of crisis, emphasizing that the industry must bear its own risks. The decision comes as regulators work to finalize rules under the GENIUS Act. This stance may have significant implications for the crypto industry's future.

title: Bitcoin VC Veterans Launch $40 Million Holding Company Targeting Small Business Acquisitions
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin VC Veterans Launch $40 Million Holding Company Targeting Small Business Acquisitions
Lyn Alden is helping debut a permanent capital company to buys profitable small businesses and put them on a Bitcoin standard.
This post Bitcoin VC Veterans Launch $40 Million Holding Company Targeting Small Business Acquisitions first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Bitcoin VC veterans have launched a $40 million holding company to acquire small businesses and transition them to a Bitcoin standard, with Lyn Alden involved in the initiative. The company aims to buy profitable small businesses, marking a new strategy in the crypto space. This move could have implications for the adoption of Bitcoin in mainstream commerce.

title: Breez Partners With Turnkey to Bring Non-Custodial Bitcoin to Backend-Run Apps
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Breez Partners With Turnkey to Bring Non-Custodial Bitcoin to Backend-Run Apps
Breez has partnered with Turnkey to let backend-run apps integrate non-custodial bitcoin wallets at scale, allowing users to retain control of their funds without requiring companies to hold private keys or redesign their existing infrastructure.
This post Breez Partners With Turnkey to Bring Non-Custodial Bitcoin to Backend-Run Apps first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Breez has partnered with Turnkey to enable backend-run apps to integrate non-custodial bitcoin wallets, allowing users to control their funds without companies holding private keys. This partnership allows for scalability and doesn't require infrastructure redesign. Users can now have more control over their bitcoin wallets.

title: Cloudflare x402 Integration Opens Door for Bitcoin in AI Agent Micropayments
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Cloudflare x402 Integration Opens Door for Bitcoin in AI Agent Micropayments
Cloudflare's adoption of the x402 protocol enables websites to monetize data for AI agents via direct crypto payments, with Bitcoin and Lightning Network positioned as strong options alongside stablecoins for privacy and speed.
This post Cloudflare x402 Integration Opens Door for Bitcoin in AI Agent Micropayments first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.
summary: Cloudflare's adoption of the x402 protocol allows websites to monetize data for AI agents using crypto payments, with Bitcoin and Lightning Network being notable options. This integration enables direct payments for data, prioritizing privacy and speed. The x402 protocol opens up new possibilities for Bitcoin in AI agent micropayments.

title: Senate Unanimously Votes That Sam Bankman-Fried Should Never Get a Pardon
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Senate Unanimously Votes That Sam Bankman-Fried Should Never Get a Pardon
The Senate unanimously approved a nonbinding resolution declaring that former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried should "under no circumstances" receive a presidential pardon or commutation.

summary: The US Senate has unanimously voted for a nonbinding resolution stating that former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried should not receive a presidential pardon or commutation. This decision comes after recent events surrounding FTX. The resolution is nonbinding, but it shows the Senate's stance on the matter.

title: Traders Took $8.2 Million From Polymarket’s Five-Minute Bitcoin Bets, Study Found
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Traders Took $8.2 Million From Polymarket’s Five-Minute Bitcoin Bets, Study Found
A new study found that Polymarket's five-minute Bitcoin contract functioned as a wealth transfer mechanism, funneling money from retail traders to a small group of manipulators while distorting Bitcoin's spot price.
This post Traders Took $8.2 Million From Polymarket’s Five-Minute Bitcoin Bets, Study Found first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: A study found that Polymarket's five-minute Bitcoin contract transferred $8.2 million from retail traders to a small group of manipulators, distorting Bitcoin's spot price. The contract functioned as a wealth transfer mechanism, favoring a few at the expense of many. This highlights a significant issue in cryptocurrency trading.

title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
content: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. GPT-Red acts as a sparring partner to boost the defenses of OpenAI's models. This breakthrough aims to enhance the safety of OpenAI's models.

title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
content: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six feet tall and connected to a supply of liquid helium that keeps them only a few degrees above absolute zero.…
summary: PsiQuantum is building a massive quantum computer using light, which will be housed in a large room with 100 stainless-steel cabinets kept near absolute zero using liquid helium. This machine has the potential to change the world. The project is still in development, but its implications are significant.

title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s looking into whether AI models can feel pain, for example,…
summary: Anthropic, the world's most valuable AI company, has published strange and heady research, including a study on whether AI models can feel pain. The company's latest discovery has garnered attention, but its implications are still being debated. Anthropic's research pushes the boundaries of AI capabilities and understanding.

title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts The AI firm Anthropic has got the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they…
summary: Anthropic has gained insight into the inner workings of large language models, including Claude, by finding a hidden space where they process concepts. This discovery sheds light on how these models think and understand information. The finding is significant for the development of AI technology.

title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
content: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

This feature enables users to produce custom videos from prompts and reference images. The integration of AI avatars enhances the video creation experience.

summary: Roblox has launched a new AI-powered game creation feature called Build, allowing users to generate basic games using a single text prompt in its mobile app. This feature simplifies the game development process, making it more accessible to users. The AI technology enables fast and easy creation of games with minimal effort.

title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
content: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.
summary: Google has updated its AI Mode to enable interaction with select apps, allowing users to complete tasks beyond just answering questions. This expansion aims to integrate AI functionality into everyday app usage. The update enhances the capabilities of Google's AI Mode.

title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
content: Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he believes visual AI is one of the next major frontiers in artificial intelligence.
summary: A former DeepMind researcher, Andrew Dai, has raised $300M at a pre-seed valuation for his new venture, focused on visual AI, which he believes is a major frontier in artificial intelligence. His experience includes helping build influential AI systems that informed the development of ChatGPT. The venture has yet to launch a product.

title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
content: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

This development may have significant implications for the AI community. Kimi 3's release is highly anticipated.

title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
content: The deal, which was rumored to be in the works last year, marks an important step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.
summary: Apple's AI ambitions in China have taken a significant step forward with the approval of Apple Intelligence for launch in the country, partnering with Alibaba and Baidu. This move is crucial for Apple's expansion in a key market. The launch is a result of a deal rumored to be in the works last year.

title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
content: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

This move suggests Microsoft is trying to gain an edge in the AI market. Microsoft's strategy focuses on highlighting the benefits of its own models.

title: Hack suggests AI music generator Suno scraped YouTube for training data
content: The hacker used an employee's credentials to access source code, which revealed how Suno scraped decades of audio.
summary: A hacker accessed the source code of AI music generator Suno, revealing that the platform scraped decades of audio from YouTube for training data. This suggests that Suno may have used copyrighted material without permission. The incident raises concerns about data privacy and ownership in AI development.

title: Microsoft patches record number of security vulnerabilities, citing its use of AI
content: Microsoft's monthly release of security fixes, dubbed Patch Tuesday, resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across the company's product line, thanks to discoveries with AI.
summary: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities with the help of AI discoveries. The company's use of AI led to the identification of a large number of security issues. This highlights the growing importance of AI in cybersecurity.

title: Inside Ode with Anthropic, the startup betting AI services are the future of enterprise
content: Can a handful of engineers really do the work of an army of consultants? That’s the bet behind Ode with Anthropic — the joint venture dedicated to embedding forward-deployed engineers in enterprise firms, backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs and others.   On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca Bellan sits down with Ode’s leaders Chris Taylor and Eddie Siegel, who founded Fractional AI, […]
summary: Ode with Anthropic is a joint venture that embeds engineers in enterprise firms to provide AI services, backed by investors like Anthropic and Goldman Sachs. The company aims to disrupt the traditional consulting model with a smaller team of engineers. Ode's leaders discuss their vision on the Equity podcast.

title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
content:
New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she's not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg's Odd Lots podcast, Hochul said that her team is using "AI to analyze every single rule, regulation, [and] policy" to check for outdated legislation.
Some of the antiquated laws mentioned by Hochul in the interview include a $25 fee required to take a dog hunting, or a stipulation that pregnant people need a permit to work after midnight. Hochul added that it "probably would have taken five years at the staff level" to review all of the laws in the stat …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: New York Governor Kathy Hochul is utilizing AI to review and analyze the state's rules and regulations to identify outdated legislation, including a $25 fee for taking a dog hunting and a permit requirement for pregnant people working after midnight. This effort aims to streamline and modernize the state's laws. The use of AI is expected to significantly reduce the time required to review the laws.

title: Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you
content:
1Password has launched a new browser integration for Claude that allows the Anthropic chatbot to access stored security credentials like usernames and passwords. The 1Password for Claude feature means that users can authorize Claude to complete multi-step tasks like booking travel and managing online accounts on their behalf without having to manually input their login credentials, but without actually exposing security information to Anthropic's AI models, according to 1Password.
That's made possible by a new "zero-exposure security framework" developed by 1Password, which works by injecting the required credentials for each task through a …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: 1Password has launched a browser integration for Claude, an Anthropic chatbot, allowing it to access stored security credentials to complete tasks without exposing sensitive information. This is made possible by 1Password's new zero-exposure security framework. Claude can now perform tasks like booking travel and managing online accounts on behalf of users.

title: Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe
content:
Google must give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to key parts of Android and Google Search after the European Union ordered the company to comply with the bloc's digital antitrust rules. 
The two decisions, handed down Thursday, could weaken Google's control over two of the tech industry's most important platforms and have far-reaching consequences for the company, shape the future of its AI tool Gemini, and open up new opportunities for rivals to gain ground. Google has until January 2027 to begin sharing search data and July 2027 to implement changes to Android.
The rulings stem from technical regulatory proceedings …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The European Union has ordered Google to give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to Android and Google Search, potentially weakening Google's control over these platforms. This move could have far-reaching consequences for the company and create new opportunities for rivals. Google must comply with the EU's digital antitrust rules by January and July 2027.

title: Computer cops
content:
I stood before a hulking glass and brick structure in the heart of Fort Worth, Texas. Thousands gathered inside to see what had been billed as "the future of policing in the digital age." As press, I was prohibited from entering, but from a number of nearby locations, I met with attendees who told me what was being sold within. And I learned that AI is threatening to seize the very heart of policing in America.
The promise of AI at this year's International Association of Chiefs of Police (IACP) Technology Conference focused on automating routine parts of the job, which also happen to be critical steps in the legal process. It's a similar …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The International Association of Chiefs of Police Technology Conference showcased AI's potential to automate routine policing tasks, which are critical to the legal process. Attendees learned about AI's role in the future of policing in America. The conference highlighted AI's impact on the digital age of policing.

title: xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM ‘deepfakes’
content:
The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM). In a lawsuit reported earlier by Reuters, xAI claims Terry Wayne Harwood "knowingly and intentionally used Grok to circumvent safeguards, alter nonconsensual images, and generate and distribute CSAM," breaching the company's policies.
Harwood was arrested in February for allegedly possessing and distributing CSAM and is facing eight felony charges. The lawsuit claims "at least some" of the images related to Harwood's criminal charges "were generated or altered" with Grok, and that Harwood byp …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: xAI is suing a man for using its Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material, claiming he breached the company's policies by circumventing safeguards and altering nonconsensual images. The man, Terry Wayne Harwood, is facing eight felony charges for possessing and distributing CSAM. The lawsuit alleges that some of the images related to Harwood's charges were generated or altered with Grok.

title: Suno snatched millions of songs from YouTube, Genius, and Deezer
content:
So when is fair use actually just stealing? | Cath Virginia / The Verge | Photo from Getty Images	

Suno data obtained in a hacking incident has exposed that the AI music generator was trained by scraping millions of songs and lyrics from online audio platforms, including YouTube Music, Deezer, and Genius, 404 Media reports. Given that Suno has avoided revealing what's in its training datasets and how they were acquired, this a rare glimpse into what Suno has actually been taking from online platforms.
That's relevant because Suno has been the subject of several lawsuits that allege it used copyrighted materials to train its AI models. In a notable case filed by the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA), Suno openly admitted th …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Suno, an AI music generator, was trained using millions of songs and lyrics scraped from YouTube, Deezer, and Genius, which has sparked controversy and lawsuits over copyright infringement. The data was obtained through a hacking incident, revealing Suno's secret training datasets. This has significant implications for the AI music generation industry and copyright laws.

title: OpenAI may announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year
content:
OpenAI's first device is set to be a smart speaker that lets you talk with ChatGPT, according to a report from Bloomberg. The device apparently won't have a screen, but will use a camera and additional sensors to "understand" your environment.
The report comes just days after Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI that accused the AI company of stealing hardware secrets. OpenAI, in a new statement on Tuesday, said that it is "not aware of any evidence that this complaint has merit."
Sources tell Bloomberg that OpenAI's device will also feature a rechargeable battery that will allow users to carry it with them. It will offer smart home contro …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI may release a ChatGPT smart speaker this year, allowing users to interact with the AI through voice commands, with features like a camera and sensors to understand the environment. The device will have a rechargeable battery and offer smart home control. This news comes amid a lawsuit from Apple accusing OpenAI of stealing hardware secrets.

title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
content: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.
summary: The EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android, which Google claims could compromise user privacy and security. This move is part of the EU's efforts to regulate big tech companies. The changes aim to promote competition and transparency in the tech industry.

This response indicates a strong stance in favor of AI integration in Linux. The decision may have significant implications for the future of Linux development.

title: Now, defenders are embracing the prompt injection, too
content: "Context bombing" tricks hacking agents into shutting down before they can do harm.
summary: Defenders are using a technique called 'context bombing' or 'prompt injection' to trick hacking agents into shutting down before they can cause harm. This method involves providing misleading context to the hacking agents. The technique is showing promise in preventing cyber attacks.

Bitcoin has also seen a 4% increase, but other cryptocurrencies like solana, TRON, and hyperliquid have decreased. This trend suggests a targeted investment in ether rather than a broader market rally.

summary: Jesse Pollak, leader of Coinbase's Base app, is stepping down after admitting his crypto social strategy failed, and will be replaced by Jordan Fish, also known as 'Cobie'. The change in leadership may impact the app's future direction. The Base app is a blockchain-based social network.

summary: Anton Bukov, co-founder of 1inch, claims he was fired in 2025 after advocating for changes in the company's management and operations. He is now moving on to a new venture. The details of his new project are not specified in the report.

summary: Bitcoin price fell 1.5% due to a reversal in US stocks, as markets shifted from bullish US inflation cues to retail profit-taking. This occurred after Micron losses surpassed 30%. The dip in Bitcoin price is linked to the overall market sentiment.

title: Gambling on random Pokémon cards: Onchain gagcha hits record high as crypto sinks

summary: Users spent a record $324 million on onchain gacha in June, despite Bitcoin hitting a 21-month low, driven by the thrill of scoring rare Pokémon cards. This trend suggests a growing interest in onchain gaming. The investment in onchain gacha is a notable development in the crypto space.

summary: Multicoin Capital has invested in Trasia, an Asia-focused trading platform built on Hyperliquid, co-founded by former Multicoin Capital partner Mable Jiang. Trasia aims to serve Asian traders. The investment marks Multicoin Capital's first in the Hyperliquid ecosystem.

summary: Keyrock, a Brussels-based firm, has closed a deal to acquire BlockFills' institutional trading and brokerage assets for $3.25 million. This acquisition was part of BlockFills' Chapter 11 bankruptcy process. The deal highlights a significant transaction in the crypto space.

title: Bitcoin to $40,000? If History’s Anything to Go By, It’s Possible, Says Report
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Magazine

title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
content: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.
summary: DoorDash has introduced a command-line tool called dd-cli, allowing developers and AI agents to search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal. title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
content:
Google is giving its AI note-taking app a new name. The company announced on Thursday that NotebookLM is becoming Gemini Notebook, but will remain a standalone app even as it integrates more deeply across Gemini and Google Search.
Google first revealed Gemini Notebook - then called Project Tailwind - in May 2023 before widely releasing the app just months later. Over the past few years, Google has been adding new features to the app to help organize and make sense of your notes, such as the ability to summarize them as AI podcasts, narrated slideshows, and TikTok-style clips. It recently started letting users connect their notebooks to the  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google is renaming its AI note-taking app from NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, with the app to remain standalone despite deeper integration with Gemini and Google Search. The app has been updated with features to organize notes, including summarizing them as AI podcasts and clips. The name change follows the app's wide release after its initial reveal in May 2023.

title: AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
content:
This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of The Odyssey. It's on track to rake in anywhere between $80-$100 million in just a few days. People are clearly excited to see how Nolan uses cutting-edge filmmaking tech to make the Homeric classic feel fresh. But another director is trying to capitalize on the buzz around Nolan's project to drum up interest in an Odysseus-focused movie of his own.
On Tuesday, film studio Fountain 0 announced that it is working on an AI-generated reimagining of The Odyssey titled Odysseus: The Fall, which will be availabl …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: A film studio is creating an AI-generated reimagining of The Odyssey, titled Odysseus: The Fall, to capitalize on the buzz around Christopher Nolan's adaptation. This move is seen as a new type of direct-to-video cash grab. The project utilizes cutting-edge AI tech in filmmaking.

title: OpenAI finally launches hardware… for Codex
content:
OpenAI is finally releasing some hardware. No, it isn't the mysterious AI-powered device the company is developing with former Apple designer Jony Ive, a project already tangled up in a messy lawsuit. Instead, it's a product designed to be used with its coding platform, Codex. 
The device, a square-shaped block of buttons called Codex Micro, is a collaboration between the AI company and keyboard maker Work Louder. OpenAI said it is a limited-run collaboration that will give users more ways to monitor and manage their agents. 
The pad closely resembles Work Louder's Creator Micro 2, and marketing images show what appears to be an identical  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI has released a new hardware product called Codex Micro, a square-shaped block of buttons designed for use with its coding platform Codex. The device is a limited-run collaboration with keyboard maker Work Louder. It aims to provide users with more ways to monitor and manage their agents.

title: Energy IPOs surge as investors hunt for ways to play AI boom
content: Companies coming to market are raising money at fastest pace this century.
summary: Energy companies are going public at a rapid pace, raising funds at the fastest rate this century, as investors seek to capitalize on the AI boom. This surge in energy IPOs indicates a growing interest in the sector, driven by the potential of AI to transform the industry. The trend is expected to continue, with more energy companies planning to go public in the near future.

title: OpenAI's first branded hardware is... a light-up keyboard?
content: The Codex Micro is designed to monitor multiple agentic threads at a glance.
summary: OpenAI has released its first branded hardware, a light-up keyboard called the Codex Micro, designed to monitor multiple agentic threads at a glance. This unusual product may indicate the company's interest in exploring new areas beyond software. The Codex Micro's purpose and functionality are not yet fully understood.

title: US military sent explosive drone boats into combat for the first time
content: US military’s drone boats struck an Iranian naval port as war heats up again.
summary: The US military has used explosive drone boats in combat for the first time, targeting an Iranian naval port amidst escalating tensions. This marks a new development in naval warfare technology. The use of drone boats could have significant implications for future conflicts.

title: Google revamps image search for its 25th anniversary with more images and more AI
content: The new Google image search will use your "unique interests" to create an always-updated gallery.
summary: Google is revamping its image search for its 25th anniversary, using more AI to create personalized galleries based on users' unique interests. The new feature will provide an always-updated gallery of images. This update aims to improve user experience with more relevant results.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
content: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.
summary: The EU has warned Meta that it may face massive fines if it doesn't disable auto-play and infinite scroll on its platforms, as part of the Digital Services Act. This act may force Meta to make significant changes to its platforms. The regulation aims to improve user experience and reduce harm.

title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
content: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.
summary: Google has updated Android Bench with new large language models (LLMs), but Gemini still lags behind. This update allows developers to contribute to the evolution of Android Bench. The introduction of new LLMs aims to improve performance and efficiency.

title: Live updates: Bitcoin holding $64,000 as AI momentum stocks continue to tumble

summary: Blockchain investigator ZachXBT strongly criticized hardware wallets, calling them 'complete garbage.' Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) remained steady near $65,000 following a recent interest rate hike in South Korea.

summary: Summer Mersinger, CEO of the Blockchain Association, advocates for the Clarity Act, deeming it the most significant consumer protection initiative in years. She asserts that consumers should not have to endure another crisis to receive the protections they deserve.

content: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Robinhood Chain memecoin launchpad Vlad.fun halts after ‘internal integrity’ issue
content: Vlad.fun suspended its platform after discovering a “serious internal integrity issue” involving members of its team, without disclosing the nature of the alleged misconduct.
summary: Vlad.fun, a memecoin launchpad operating on Robinhood Chain, has suspended its platform. The halt was caused by a "serious internal integrity issue" involving members of its team. The specific details of the alleged misconduct have not been disclosed.

title: The privacy paradox of protecting kids online
content: We don’t need to imagine the privacy pitfalls of age verification. They’ve been happening for years, argues Cardano Foundation CEO Frederik Gregaard.
summary: The article addresses the long-standing privacy challenges inherent in age verification systems designed to protect children online. Frederik Gregaard, CEO of the Cardano Foundation, contends that these privacy pitfalls have been apparent for many years.

title: Trump teleprompter operator made $100K betting on Kalshi markets tied to speeches: ABC
content: According to ABC News, federal regulators are investigating whether the longtime White House staffer used nonpublic information to profit from event contracts tied to President Donald Trump’s speeches.
summary: Federal regulators are investigating a longtime White House staffer, a Trump teleprompter operator, for allegedly using nonpublic information. The staffer reportedly made $100,000 by betting on Kalshi event contracts tied to President Donald Trump's speeches. The probe focuses on potential insider trading.

title: MegaETH sunsets Mega Mafia accelerator program, noting ‘most’ of its successful apps left
content: MegaETH did not take equity or governance positions in the approximately 20 projects incubated in the Mega Mafia.
summary: MegaETH has ended its Mega Mafia accelerator program, which supported around 20 projects, with most of the successful apps having already left the program. The program did not take equity or governance positions in the projects. This move marks the conclusion of the initiative.

title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
content: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.
summary: SpaceX's stock price has fallen to its IPO price of $135 ahead of the Starship launch, indicating that markets are reassessing the promises made by CEO Elon Musk. The stock had previously experienced a post-IPO high, but has since steadily declined. This development may impact investor confidence in the company.

title: Trump to meet with senators over CLARITY Act on Thursday: Politico
content: US President Donald Trump is set to meet with several senators on Thursday as negotiators race to get the CLARITY Act across the line before the Senate’s August recess.
summary: US President Donald Trump is meeting with senators to discuss the CLARITY Act, which negotiators are trying to pass before the Senate's August recess. The meeting is scheduled for Thursday. The outcome of the meeting may impact the Act's progress.

title: Why heat pumps are still so hot in the US
content: It feels as if it should be illegal to even think about heating appliances during the height of summer—seriously, these heat waves in New York have been brutal—but we need to talk about heat pumps. The appliances use electricity for heating, they’re incredibly efficient, and they’re on the rise. (For what it’s worth, many heat…
summary: The article discusses the increasing popularity of heat pumps in the US, highlighting their efficiency and rise in usage. Heat pumps use electricity for heating and are becoming more prominent. The topic is unrelated to AI or crypto, focusing on home appliances.

title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
content: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the Netherlands banned anonymous donation in 2004, the doctor who ran the clinic destroyed records that might have identified those donors, he says. He…
summary: A European fertility group is calling for limits on sperm donors, citing concerns for individuals conceived through anonymous donation, like 47-year-old Ties van der Meer, who doesn't know how many siblings he has. The issue arose after a clinic in the Netherlands destroyed donor records. This affects people's ability to know their genetic lineage.
